summary: Applied the identity taxonomy live by re-reviewing the current human, service, agent, and break-glass principals against the running Proxmox and mail-platform surfaces.

verification.observed: Routine Linux access still lands on ops through the step-ca-backed SSH path, and ops@pam remains present with a TOTP factor for routine Proxmox administration.
The lv3-automation@pve principal and privilege-separated primary token remain present, and token-auth verification against the Proxmox version endpoint returned 9.1.6.
The private mail gateway mailbox inventory still includes server@lv3.org as the managed service mailbox on docker-runtime-lv3.
The host SSH daemon still reports permitrootlogin without-password and passwordauthentication no, which keeps root in key-only break-glass mode.

notes: The shared bootstrap-key debt between ops and root remains explicitly documented in canonical state even though routine operator access now uses short-lived step-ca SSH certificates.
This live apply records the taxonomy as active operator policy; it does not introduce a new identity provider.
